headline: Samsung and SK Hynix tumble 7%+ as chip rout spreads
detail: This is a direct hit to your book. Samsung and SK Hynix are bellwethers for the entire semiconductor supply chain - when they drop this hard, it signals broad sector fear, not just company-specific news. SMH, SOXL, and any chip-adjacent names you hold will feel this at the US open.
rec: No new buys in chip names yet. Watch SMH's behavior at open - if it undercuts recent lows on volume, SOXL's $200 stop becomes urgent. Protect first, consider entries only after stabilization.

headline: BYD and Xiaomi surge on strong June delivery figures
detail: Strong EV delivery data out of China signals consumer demand holding up in that market, which is a modest positive for chip demand broadly (EVs are chip-intensive). However, this is a Chinese consumer story, not a direct catalyst for your US-listed semi holdings today given the offsetting Samsung/Hynix rout.
rec: Nothing to act on here for your specific book - the positive demand signal is too indirect to trade against a live chip sector selloff.

headline: Oil falls as Middle East tensions ease - worst quarter for Brent since 2020
detail: Lower oil is a quiet positive for tech - it reduces input costs and eases inflation fears, which is supportive for growth stock valuations and could take pressure off the Fed. For your QQQ and SPY positions, this is a mild tailwind.
rec: Your QQQ and SPY are both HOLD with comfortable distance to stops ($670 and $705 respectively) - no action needed, just a background positive to note.

headline: Ant Group rushes into humanoid robots with a dozen deals in 18 months
detail: Ant Group aggressively building out humanoid robot capabilities signals accelerating AI and robotics investment in China, which broadly supports chip demand narratives. However, this is a medium-term thematic story with no immediate price catalyst for your holdings today.
rec: No trade today - file this as confirmation that AI hardware demand narratives remain intact longer term, which is supportive context for your chip names once the current rout stabilizes.
